rationale: Two actionable patch findings. CR-001: emailSlug regex chain is duplicated verbatim between report-generator.ts (private function) and session-report.ts (inline copy) — any future slug change must be applied in two places and can silently diverge. CR-002: both email read-sites use '?? undefined', which passes an empty string through into report meta as userEmail: '' while the filename slug check (falsy for '') adds no prefix — metadata and filename become inconsistent. All 6 acceptance criteria are satisfied. Deferred: NaN-guard on numeric timestamps (EC-002, unlikely corrupt-data case), and HTML UI visibility of new fields (AC-F02, outside current plan scope — fields are present in the embedded data blob).

findings.problem: emailSlug logic is duplicated verbatim in session-report.ts (inline) rather than imported from report-generator.ts. The function in report-generator.ts is private (not exported), which forced the copy.
findings.impact: Any future change to the slug algorithm (length cap, Unicode normalisation, edge-case fix) must be applied in two places. The two implementations can silently diverge, producing different filename patterns from the CLI analytics path and the per-session auto-report path for the same email address.
findings.recommendation: Export emailSlug from report-generator.ts (`export function emailSlug(email: string): string`) and import it in session-report.ts, replacing the inline regex chain.

findings.problem: In analytics/index.ts and BaseAgentAdapter.ts, `userEmail = cfg.userEmail ?? undefined` passes an empty string through unchanged. When userEmail is '' the condition `ctx.userEmail !== undefined` is true so '' is spread into report meta as `userEmail: ''`. Meanwhile the filename slug check `userEmail ?` is falsy for '', so no slug is prepended — the file has no email slug but the embedded JSON has `userEmail: ''`.
findings.impact: Metadata and filename are inconsistent. Downstream consumers treating userEmail presence as evidence of a known identity see a blank string rather than an absent field. Automated tools parsing filenames to correlate with report owner would fail to find a match.
findings.recommendation: Use `userEmail = cfg.userEmail || undefined` (and `userEmail = profileConfig.userEmail || undefined` in BaseAgentAdapter) at both read sites to normalise empty strings to undefined.

business_review.criterion: ReportMeta extended with optional userEmail?, periodStart?, periodEnd?
business_review.notes: All three optional fields added to ReportMeta in types.ts with correct types and inline doc comments.

business_review.criterion: PayloadContext extended; buildPayload maps new fields conditionally
business_review.notes: PayloadContext in payload-builder.ts gains all three fields; buildPayload spreads them using !== undefined guards.

business_review.criterion: getDefaultReportPath/JsonPath accept optional email; slug prepended before date when present
business_review.notes: Private emailSlug() helper added; both exports accept userEmail?; slug is inserted between 'codemie-analytics-' and the date.

business_review.criterion: SessionReportOptions.outputPath optional; userEmail? added; periodStart/periodEnd extracted from session events
business_review.notes: outputPath now optional with computed default; userEmail? added; period extracted from rawSessions[0] start/end events.

business_review.criterion: BaseAgentAdapter extracts email from CODEMIE_PROFILE_CONFIG; hardcoded outputPath removed
business_review.notes: JSON.parse with try/catch; email passed to generateSessionReport; hardcoded outputPath construction removed.

business_review.criterion: runAnalytics loads email from ConfigLoader; passes userEmail, periodStart, periodEnd to buildPayload; passes email to default path helpers
business_review.notes: ConfigLoader statically imported; email loaded via try/catch; all three fields conditionally spread; all default-path calls receive userEmail.

standards_review.notes: Explicit return types on all exported functions, .js extensions on all imports, proper optional typing, no any.
emailSlug strips all non-alphanumeric chars, preventing path traversal. JSON.parse guarded with try/catch. No secrets or credentials introduced.
